item.title: Version 20.1
item.description:
A big stability and polish release — the headline fix is the iPhone playback freeze on the Native engine, plus a major overhaul of resume, media‑server reliability, the guide, and loading indicators.

Playback

  • Fixed the periodic video freeze on iPhone — live channels on the Native engine could freeze for ~a second every few minutes while audio kept playing. The engine also now auto‑recovers when a server drops the connection.
  • The Native engine works again over plain‑HTTP on iOS 26, restoring the higher‑quality deinterlacer for sports and interlaced channels.
  • Sheds expensive image processing when the device gets hot, so long sessions stutter less.
  • Assorted player fixes (volume‑slider drag, catch‑up timing, engine selection).

Watch progress & resume

  • Resume actually works now across movies and series, on IPTV and Emby/Plex/Jellyfin — progress is saved reliably during playback.
  • Continue Watching is sorted by most‑recent; “Start from Beginning” starts immediately.
  • Fixed progress routing with multiple media servers; Plex now reports mid‑playback.

Media servers & artwork

  • Fixed Jellyfin opening the Emby view, and out‑of‑order Emby/Jellyfin refreshes dropping content.
  • More accurate posters via release‑year matching; a loading shimmer while they fetch and a progress bar during first‑time artwork setup.

Guide & interface

  • Much lighter EPG handling — large guides no longer contend with playback, and failing sources back off instead of retrying every launch.
  • Loading indicators throughout (counts, provider lists, libraries, home) instead of blank/empty on launch.
  • Full Dynamic Type pass; proper iPad split‑view browse layout.
  • Credentials in stream URLs are now redacted from logs.

Requires macOS 14 or later. Universal (Apple Silicon + Intel).

item.title: Version 20.1
item.description:
A big stability and polish release — the headline fix is the iPhone playback freeze on the Native engine, plus a major overhaul of resume, media‑server reliability, the guide, and loading indicators.

Playback

  • Fixed the periodic video freeze on iPhone — live channels on the Native engine could freeze for ~a second every few minutes while audio kept playing. The engine also now auto‑recovers when a server drops the connection.
  • The Native engine works again over plain‑HTTP on iOS 26, restoring the higher‑quality deinterlacer for sports and interlaced channels.
  • Sheds expensive image processing when the device gets hot, so long sessions stutter less.
  • Assorted player fixes (volume‑slider drag, catch‑up timing, engine selection).

Watch progress & resume

  • Resume actually works now across movies and series, on IPTV and Emby/Plex/Jellyfin — progress is saved reliably during playback.
  • Continue Watching is sorted by most‑recent; “Start from Beginning” starts immediately.
  • Fixed progress routing with multiple media servers; Plex now reports mid‑playback.

Media servers & artwork

  • Fixed Jellyfin opening the Emby view, and out‑of‑order Emby/Jellyfin refreshes dropping content.
  • More accurate posters via release‑year matching; a loading shimmer while they fetch and a progress bar during first‑time artwork setup.

Guide & interface

  • Much lighter EPG handling — large guides no longer contend with playback, and failing sources back off instead of retrying every launch.
  • Loading indicators throughout (counts, provider lists, libraries, home) instead of blank/empty on launch.
  • Full Dynamic Type pass; proper iPad split‑view browse layout.
  • Credentials in stream URLs are now redacted from logs.

Requires macOS 14 or later. Universal (Apple Silicon + Intel).
